title: Identify System Users, Processes, and Devices
description: Identify information system users, processes acting on behalf of users, and devices.

checks.name: All User Accounts Have Unique Identifiers
checks.purpose: Verify all local and domain user accounts have unique SIDs and usernames with no shared or generic accounts present

checks.name: Service Accounts Have Named Identities
checks.purpose: Confirm all services and scheduled tasks run under named service accounts rather than SYSTEM, NetworkService, or anonymous identities

checks.name: Device Identity is Established
checks.purpose: Verify the system has a unique computer account and is domain joined or registered with an identity management system

checks.name: All User Accounts Have Unique Identifiers
checks.purpose: Verify all local and domain user accounts have unique SIDs and usernames with no shared or generic accounts present

checks.name: Service Accounts Have Named Identities
checks.purpose: Confirm all services and scheduled tasks run under named service accounts or gMSAs rather than SYSTEM or anonymous identities

checks.name: Device Identity is Established
checks.purpose: Verify the server has a unique computer account in Active Directory and is domain joined

checks.name: No Duplicate or Orphaned Accounts Exist
checks.purpose: Confirm no orphaned accounts exist for departed users and all accounts are traceable to an authorized identity record

checks.name: All User Accounts Have Unique UIDs
checks.purpose: Verify no duplicate UIDs exist in /etc/passwd and all accounts have unique named identifiers

checks.name: Service Accounts Have Named Identities
checks.purpose: Confirm all systemd services declare a User= directive and no daemons run as root unnecessarily

checks.name: No Generic or Anonymous Accounts Exist
checks.purpose: Verify no accounts named guest, temp, test, or shared exist with login shells in /etc/passwd

checks.name: All User Accounts Have Unique UIDs
checks.purpose: Verify no duplicate UIDs exist in /etc/passwd and all accounts have unique named identifiers

checks.name: Service Accounts Have Named Identities
checks.purpose: Confirm all systemd services declare a User= directive and no daemons run as root unnecessarily

checks.name: No Generic or Anonymous Accounts Exist
checks.purpose: Verify no accounts named guest, temp, test, or shared exist with login shells in /etc/passwd

remediation: Assign unique usernames and identifiers to all users. Rename or disable generic accounts. Assign named service accounts to all services and scheduled tasks. Domain-join all managed systems. On Linux, remove or lock accounts with generic names and assign User= directives to all systemd services.
